title: BiRAGAS, Inc.
headline: Simple Agreement for Future Equity (SAFE).
subhead: Discount, No Valuation Cap. With a milestone-gated 12-month Refund Right rider. Document date: May 13, 2026.
narration: BiRAGAS, Incorporated. A Texas corporation, in formation. Term Sheet for a Simple Agreement for Future Equity — SAFE. Discount, No Valuation Cap. With a milestone-gated twelve-month Refund Right rider. Version one-point-one R. Confidential.

title: About this Document
headline: A non-binding summary of the principal terms.
subhead: Except for Confidentiality, Expenses, and Governing Law — which are binding upon execution — no party has any legal obligation under this Term Sheet until the actual SAFE and Definitive Documents are executed. The signing SAFE is based on the Y Combinator Post-Money Discount-No-Cap template, modified for Texas governance and Company-specific provisions.
narration: About this document. This is a non-binding summary of the principal terms. Except for confidentiality, expenses, and governing law — which become binding upon execution — no party has any legal obligation under this Term Sheet until the actual SAFE and Definitive Documents are executed. The signing SAFE is based on the Y Combinator Post-Money Discount-No-Cap template, modified for Texas governance and Company-specific provisions.

title: Issuer & Sponsor
headline: BiRAGAS, Inc. — Texas corporation, in formation.
subhead: Ayass Bioscience, LLC will contribute and assign all intellectual property, source code, model weights, and related assets comprising the BiRAGAS platform (the BiRAGAS IP) to the Company in exchange for Founders Common Stock concurrently with the first SAFE close.

round.v: BiRAGAS, Inc. — Texas corporation (in formation)
Ayass Bioscience, LLC — Texas LLC
All BiRAGAS platform IP → Founders Common Stock
Unpriced rolling SAFE — each Investor signs an individual SAFE

narration: Issuer and Sponsor. BiRAGAS Incorporated, a Texas corporation, is being formed concurrently with the first SAFE closing. Ayass Bioscience, LLC, a Texas limited liability company, will contribute and assign all intellectual property, source code, model weights, and related assets comprising the BiRAGAS platform to the Company in exchange for Founders Common Stock concurrently with that first close.

title: The Security
headline: YC Post-Money SAFE. Discount, No Valuation Cap.
subhead: The signing document is the Y Combinator Post-Money SAFE template — Discount, No Valuation Cap version — modified for Texas governance and Company-specific provisions including the Performance Milestones & Refund Right described later in this deck.

round.v: Simple Agreement for Future Equity (SAFE)
YC Post-Money · Discount, No Valuation Cap
Texas governance + Refund Right rider (Side Letter)

narration: The security. The Simple Agreement for Future Equity is the Y Combinator Post-Money SAFE template, in its Discount, No Valuation Cap version, modified for Texas governance and Company-specific provisions including the Performance Milestones and Refund Right described later in this deck.

title: Subscription
headline: Minimum $250,000. No fixed maximum. Rolling close.
subhead: Subscriptions accepted from qualified Accredited Investors on a rolling basis. The Sponsor reserves the right to accept, reject, or limit any subscription in its sole discretion. The minimum subscription per SAFE is waivable.

round.v: USD $250,000 per Investor (waivable)
No fixed maximum — Sponsor discretion
Not fixed — rolling acceptance of commitments
Qualified Accredited Investors only

narration: Subscription. The minimum is two hundred fifty thousand US dollars per investor, waivable at Sponsor discretion. There is no fixed maximum per SAFE and no fixed total round size. Subscriptions are accepted from qualified accredited investors on a rolling basis. The Sponsor reserves the right to accept, reject, or limit any subscription in its sole discretion.

title: Economic Terms
headline: 20% discount. No valuation cap. 1.25× the shares per dollar.
subhead: The SAFE converts at twenty percent below the per-share price paid by Series A investors. By design — there is no valuation cap.

stats.l: Shares per dollar vs. Series A investors

narration: Economic terms. The headline numbers. Twenty percent discount, with a discount multiplier of zero-point-eight-zero. No valuation cap, by design. And, as a result, SAFE investors receive one-point-two-five times as many shares per dollar invested as Series A investors at the same dollar amount.

title: Conversion Math
headline: SAFE Price = Standard Preferred Price × 0.80.
subhead: At the Qualified Equity Financing, the SAFE's per-share price is set automatically at eighty percent of the price paid by the lead Series A investor.

steps.d: Per-share price set by lead Series A investor in arm's-length negotiation.
Per-share price at which SAFE converts.
Number of preferred shares issued to SAFE holder.

narration: Conversion math. The SAFE price equals the Standard Preferred price multiplied by zero-point-eight. The Standard Preferred Price is set by the lead Series A investor in arm's-length negotiation. Multiplied by the zero-point-eight Discount Multiplier, that yields the SAFE Price. The Investor's number of preferred shares equals the Purchase Amount divided by the SAFE Price.

title: Conversion Trigger
headline: Automatic conversion at the Qualified Equity Financing.
subhead: A Qualified Equity Financing means a bona fide priced equity financing of preferred stock led by an institutional investor in which the per-share price is set in arm's-length negotiation. No specific dollar threshold required. Conversion is mandatory and automatic at the closing — no further action needed by the SAFE holder.

round.v: Qualified Equity Financing (institutional-led priced round)
None — any bona fide priced round qualifies
Same Preferred Stock issued to Series A investors
Yes — no action required by SAFE holder

narration: Conversion trigger. Conversion is automatic at the Company's next Qualified Equity Financing — a bona fide priced equity financing of preferred stock, led by an institutional investor in arm's-length negotiation. No specific dollar threshold is required. At closing, each SAFE converts into shares of the same class of Preferred Stock issued to the Series A investors, with all rights, preferences, and privileges of that class.

title: Investor Protections
headline: Four pre-conversion protections.
subhead: Built into the SAFE itself — independent of any later financing.

pillars.d: On sale/IPO before conversion: cashout OR convert to common at LE valuation.
SAFE holders rank ahead of common stockholders; equal with other SAFEs.
If a later SAFE offers better terms, original SAFEs may elect them.
Right to participate in the Qualified Equity Financing at pro-rata share.

narration: Investor protections — pre-conversion. Four protections built into the SAFE itself, independent of any later financing. Liquidity Event election rights. Dissolution priority over common stockholders. Most Favored Nation protection. And pro-rata participation rights via an Anchor Investor Side Letter.

title: Liquidity Event Election
kicker: If sale or IPO happens before conversion
headline: Investor's choice: cashout or convert to common.
subhead: If a Liquidity Event — sale, change of control, or IPO — occurs before SAFE conversion, the Investor may elect, at their option: (a) CASHOUT — receive the Purchase Amount back; or (b) CONVERSION TO COMMON — convert into Common Stock at the Liquidity Event valuation divided by the Liquidity Event Capitalization.
narration: Liquidity Event election. If a Liquidity Event — sale, change of control, or IPO — occurs before SAFE conversion, the Investor may elect, at their option, between two outcomes. Option A — cashout — the Investor receives the Purchase Amount back. Option B — conversion to common — the Investor converts into Common Stock at the Liquidity Event valuation, divided by the Liquidity Event Capitalization.

title: Dissolution Priority
headline: SAFE holders rank ahead of Common.
subhead: If the Company dissolves before SAFE conversion, SAFE holders rank AHEAD OF Common Stockholders for return of the Purchase Amount, equal in priority with other SAFE holders, behind debt and secured obligations.
narration: Dissolution priority. If the Company dissolves before the SAFE has converted, SAFE holders rank ahead of common stockholders for return of the Purchase Amount, equal in priority with other SAFE holders, and behind debt and secured obligations.

title: Most Favored Nation
kicker: If a later SAFE is more generous
headline: Better terms flow back automatically.
subhead: If the Company subsequently issues another SAFE on terms more favorable to the new investor — higher discount, valuation cap, additional protections — each original SAFE investor may elect to apply those more favorable terms to its own SAFE.
narration: Most Favored Nation. If the Company subsequently issues another SAFE on terms more favorable to the new investor — for example a higher discount rate, a valuation cap, or additional protections — each original SAFE investor may elect to apply those more favorable terms to its own SAFE.

title: Pro-Rata Rights
headline: Right to maintain your share at the Series A.
subhead: Each Investor receives Pro-Rata Participation Rights via an Anchor Investor Side Letter — the right (but not the obligation) to participate in the Qualified Equity Financing on the same terms as new investors, in an amount up to the Investor's Pro-Rata share.
narration: Pro-rata rights. Each Investor receives Pro-Rata Participation Rights via an Anchor Investor Side Letter. This is the right, but not the obligation, to participate in the Qualified Equity Financing on the same terms as new investors, in an amount up to the Investor's pro-rata share.

title: Pre-Conversion Rights
kicker: What you get before the Series A
headline: Information rights. No voting. No board. No inspection.
subhead: Pre-conversion, SAFE holders receive semiannual investor updates covering operating metrics, customer wins, key hires, and material developments. All other governance rights arise upon SAFE conversion at the Qualified Equity Financing.

round.v: Semiannual investor update — metrics, customers, hires, material updates
None pre-conversion (arise at QEF)
None pre-conversion
None pre-conversion
None pre-conversion — 20% discount provides equivalent protection

narration: Pre-conversion rights. SAFE holders receive a semiannual investor update covering operating metrics, customer wins, key hires, and material developments. Inspection rights, voting rights, board representation, and anti-dilution protection are not granted pre-conversion. They arise upon SAFE conversion at the Qualified Equity Financing. The twenty percent discount provides equivalent economic protection in lieu of pre-conversion anti-dilution.

title: Post-Conversion Rights
kicker: What you get after the Series A
headline: Same rights as Series A. NVCA standard.
subhead: Upon conversion, SAFE holders receive Preferred Stock with all rights, preferences, and privileges of Series A investors — typically NVCA-standard terms.

round.v: Same Preferred Stock as Series A investors
Same as Series A — typically 1× non-participating
Same as Series A — as-converted-to-Common basis
Same as Series A — typically requiring Series A vote on major actions
Same as Series A — typically broad-based weighted average
Same as Series A — NVCA standard (Demand + S-3 + Piggyback)
Info, inspection, ROFR/Co-Sale, Drag-Along — as negotiated by the lead

narration: Post-conversion rights. Upon conversion, SAFE holders receive Preferred Stock with all rights, preferences, and privileges of Series A investors. Same class of stock. Same liquidation preference — typically one-times non-participating. Same voting rights, on an as-converted-to-common basis. Same protective provisions. Same broad-based weighted-average anti-dilution. NVCA-standard registration rights — demand, S-3, and piggyback. And other standard rights — information, inspection, right of first refusal, co-sale, and drag-along — as negotiated by the lead institutional investor in the Qualified Equity Financing.

title: Closing Mechanics
headline: Rolling close. Escrow. Sponsor discretion.
subhead: Each SAFE closes individually as Investor commitments are accepted by the Sponsor. Pending close, Purchase Amounts are held in escrow with an unaffiliated escrow agent; funds release only at close.

round.v: ROLLING — each SAFE closes individually
Q3 2027 (Sponsor may extend or terminate)
Unaffiliated escrow agent · funds release at close
Sponsor may accept, reject, or limit subscriptions in its sole discretion

narration: Closing mechanics. The round closes on a rolling basis — each SAFE closes individually as investor commitments are accepted by the Sponsor. The first closing target is Q3 twenty-twenty-six. The final closing target is Q3 twenty-twenty-seven, subject to the Sponsor's discretion to extend or terminate. Pending each close, Purchase Amounts are held in escrow with an unaffiliated escrow agent. Funds release only upon close. Subscriptions are not binding until accepted by the Sponsor in writing.

title: First Closing Conditions
kicker: Six conditions for the first SAFE close
headline: What must happen first.
subhead: These six conditions must be satisfied before the first SAFE can close — or waived by the Sponsor.

pillars.d: BiRAGAS, Inc. validly formed in Texas.
IP Contribution and Assignment Agreement executed; BiRAGAS IP transferred.
SAFE and related agreements fully executed.
Initial subscription level met (in Sponsor's discretion).
Directors and officers liability insurance bound.
Customary closing certificates delivered.

narration: First closing conditions. Six conditions must be satisfied before the first SAFE can close. One — BiRAGAS Incorporated validly formed in Texas. Two — the IP Contribution and Assignment Agreement executed and BiRAGAS IP transferred. Three — Definitive Documents executed. Four — initial subscription threshold met, in the Sponsor's discretion. Five — directors and officers insurance bound. Six — customary closing certificates delivered.

title: Use of Proceeds
headline: Capital deployed against the $4M ARR Series A trigger.
subhead: An operating plan oriented to the Qualified Equity Financing threshold. Quarterly reporting to SAFE holders maps spend back to milestone progress.
narration: Use of proceeds. SAFE Purchase Amounts are deployed against an operating plan oriented toward the four-million-dollar ARR Series A trigger. Approximately thirty to thirty-five percent to sales, marketing, and customer success. Twenty to twenty-five percent to engineering. Fifteen to twenty percent to research and development and validation. Five to ten percent to regulatory and intellectual property. Ten to fifteen percent to G and A. Ten percent to working capital and contingency. Quarterly reporting to SAFE holders maps spend back to milestone progress.

title: Performance Milestones & Refund Right
headline: If we miss the milestones, you get your money back.
subhead: Twelve months from your SAFE Effective Date, the Company must satisfy Tier-1 / Tier-2 / Tier-3 milestone thresholds. If we fall short — and no Material Adverse Event has occurred — you have a one-time, non-transferable election to receive a refund of your Purchase Amount.
narration: Performance milestones and the Refund Right. This is the section added in version one-point-one R. The plain-English summary: if we miss the milestones, you get your money back. Twelve months from your SAFE Effective Date, the Company must satisfy the Tier-one, Tier-two, and Tier-three milestone thresholds. If we fall short, and no Material Adverse Event has occurred, you have a one-time, non-transferable election to receive a refund of your Purchase Amount.

title: The Refund Mechanic
headline: Three deadlines. One election.
subhead: From your individual SAFE Effective Date — the date funds release from escrow to the Company — through final refund payment.

steps.d: Funds release from escrow to the Company.
Company prepares a Milestone Compliance Certificate signed by CEO and reviewed by auditor.
Within 60 days of period end. Investor reviews compliance.
Investor has 30 days from receipt of certificate to elect refund. Election binding and irrevocable.

narration: The refund mechanic. Three deadlines, one election. From your individual SAFE Effective Date — the date funds release from escrow to the Company — through final refund payment. Time zero. Funds release. Plus twelve months. Performance Period ends. The Company prepares a Milestone Compliance Certificate signed by the CEO and reviewed by our auditor. The certificate is delivered to investors within sixty days. From receipt of the certificate, you have thirty days to elect a refund. The election is binding and irrevocable.

title: Tier 1 — Corporate & Governance
headline: Hygiene, transparency, and IP custody.
subhead: Tier 1 confirms the Company exists, is well-governed, and still owns the platform that justifies your valuation.

pillars.d: BiRAGAS, Inc. formed under Texas law · IP Contribution & Assignment executed · D&O insurance bound · corporate records maintained.
Quarterly written investor reports on or before each fiscal quarter-end; ≥3 of 4 delivered during the Performance Period.
No transfer, assignment, encumbrance, or material loss of BiRAGAS IP outside ordinary course; Company remains sole owner or exclusive licensee of the BiRAGAS platform.

narration: Tier one. Corporate and governance. All three required. T1-A. BiRAGAS Incorporated validly formed under Texas law. The BiRAGAS IP Contribution and Assignment Agreement executed. D and O insurance bound. Customary corporate records maintained. T1-B. Quarterly written investor reports delivered on or before each fiscal quarter-end. Minimum of three of four quarterly reports delivered during the Performance Period. T1-C. No transfer, assignment, encumbrance, or material loss of the BiRAGAS IP outside the ordinary course of business. The Company remains the sole owner or exclusive licensee of the BiRAGAS platform.

title: Tier 2 — Commercial & Pipeline
headline: Revenue. Customers. Deployments. Partnership.
subhead: Any one of four commercial proof points satisfies the Tier-2 threshold.

pillars.d: Cumulative bookings from signed customer contracts (subscription, license, or service). LOIs and non-binding term sheets do not count.
Under signed contract (pilot, license, or service); ≥1 enterprise biopharma, CRO, or academic medical center.
Production or paid pilot beyond founder-controlled environments.
≥1 executed strategic partnership, JDA, or binding LOI with a pharma or diagnostic with prior-year revenue ≥ $20M.

narration: Tier two. Commercial and pipeline. At least one of four required. T2-1, cumulative bookings of at least two hundred thousand US dollars from signed customer contracts — subscription, license, or service. Letters of intent and non-binding term sheets do not count. T2-2, at least two paying customers under signed contract — pilot, license, or service — at least one of which is an enterprise biopharma, CRO, or academic medical center. T2-3, at least two executed BiRAGAS platform deployments — production or paid pilot — beyond founder-controlled environments. T2-4, at least one executed strategic partnership, joint development agreement, or binding letter of intent with a pharmaceutical or diagnostic company with prior-year revenue of at least twenty million US dollars.

title: Tier 3 — Product, IP & Validation
headline: Build. Protect. Publish.
subhead: Any one of three credibility milestones satisfies the Tier-3 threshold.

pillars.d: ≥ 20 of 28 BiRAGAS modules at production-ready status per the Company's Module Readiness Specification (filed with quarterly reports).
≥ 2 patent applications filed (provisional or non-provisional) covering BiRAGAS IP since the SAFE Effective Date.
≥ 1 peer-reviewed paper, pre-print, or regulatory dossier featuring the BiRAGAS platform — accepted or submitted to a recognized journal or agency.

narration: Tier three. Product, intellectual property, and validation. At least one of three required. T3-1, at least twenty of the twenty-eight BiRAGAS modules at production-ready status as defined in the Company's Module Readiness Specification, filed with each quarterly report. T3-2, at least two patent applications filed — provisional or non-provisional — covering BiRAGAS IP since the SAFE Effective Date. T3-3, at least one peer-reviewed publication, pre-print, or regulatory dossier featuring the BiRAGAS platform, accepted or submitted to a recognized journal or agency.

title: Material Adverse Event
headline: Four events bypass the milestone tally entirely.
subhead: If any one of these occurs during the Performance Period, the Refund Right activates regardless of how many milestones the Company has hit: (i) loss or invalidation of material BiRAGAS IP unremedied within 90 days; (ii) bankruptcy, receivership, or assignment for benefit of creditors; (iii) departure of the Founder & CEO without a Board-approved successor within 120 days; (iv) a regulatory or judicial finding that materially prevents the Company from operating the BiRAGAS platform in the United States.
narration: The override — Material Adverse Event. Four events bypass the milestone tally entirely. If any one of these occurs during the Performance Period, the Refund Right activates automatically regardless of milestone count. One — loss, invalidation, or third-party challenge of any material portion of the BiRAGAS IP that the Company fails to remedy within ninety days. Two — bankruptcy, receivership, or general assignment for the benefit of creditors. Three — departure of the Founder and CEO without a Board-approved successor in place within one hundred twenty days. Four — a regulatory or judicial finding that materially prevents the Company from operating the BiRAGAS platform in the United States.

title: Payment of Refund
headline: 90 days from valid election. Subordinated to debt + reserve.
subhead: Refunds paid out of the Company's available cash, ratably across all electing investors. The Company retains a 12-month operating reserve. Unpaid balance, if any, converts to a promissory note maturing in 18 months.

round.v: 90 days from valid election
Company's available cash, ratably across electing investors
Behind secured debt + trade creditors + 12-month operating reserve
Refund subject to applicable corporate-law solvency tests
Auto-converts to non-convertible note · AFR + 200 bps · 18-month maturity

narration: Payment of refund. Within ninety days of a valid election. Source of funds: the Company's available cash, paid ratably across all electing investors. Subordination: behind secured debt, trade creditors, and a twelve-month operating reserve as determined by the Board in good faith. Subject to applicable corporate-law solvency tests. To the extent the Company has insufficient cash to satisfy all refunds within the payment window, the unpaid balance automatically converts into a non-convertible promissory note bearing simple interest at the Applicable Federal Rate plus two hundred basis points, maturing eighteen months from issuance.

title: Termination & Extension
headline: Four ways the right terminates. One way it extends.
subhead: The Refund Right is designed to align Investor protection with Company progress. It ends when the security has effectively converted or upon the Investor's affirmative waiver.

round.v: (a) Closing of a Qualified Equity Financing
(b) Liquidity Event
(c) Missed Refund Election Window
(d) Investor's affirmative written waiver
+6 months on majority-in-interest SAFE-holder consent (Sponsor-affiliated holdings excluded)
Later-issued SAFE with better refund mechanic flows back automatically

narration: Termination and extension. The Refund Right terminates automatically upon the earliest of four events. A — closing of a Qualified Equity Financing, at which point the SAFE converts under its standard terms. B — a Liquidity Event. C — failure of the Investor to deliver a Refund Election within the election window. D — the Investor's affirmative written waiver. Extension: the Company may, with the affirmative consent of holders representing a majority-in-interest of the then-outstanding SAFE purchase amounts (excluding Sponsor-affiliated holdings), extend the Performance Period by up to six months. The Most Favored Nation provision applies — if the Company later issues a SAFE with a more favorable milestone schedule or refund mechanic, each Investor may elect to substitute those more favorable terms.

title: Tax Intent
headline: IRC §351 for the IP contribution. §1202 for the QSBS.
subhead: The Refund Right may affect both. Each Investor should consult its own tax advisor.

round.v: Intended to qualify for tax-deferred treatment under IRC §351
Intended to qualify as Qualified Small Business Stock under IRC §1202
May affect both §351 and §1202 qualification — consult tax advisor

narration: Tax intent. Two tax positions. The IP Contribution is intended to qualify for tax-deferred treatment under Internal Revenue Code section three-fifty-one. The Preferred Stock issued upon SAFE conversion is intended to qualify as Qualified Small Business Stock under Internal Revenue Code section twelve-oh-two. The Refund Right may affect both qualifications. Each Investor is encouraged to consult its own tax advisor. The Company makes no representation regarding tax outcomes.

title: Confidentiality · Expenses · Law
headline: Three provisions are binding now.
subhead: Everything else in this Term Sheet is non-binding until the Definitive Documents are executed.

round.v: Strict — except as required by law or to bound professional advisors
Each party bears its own; Company does not reimburse Investor legal
Texas
Texas Court of Chancery (internal affairs) · Texas state/federal (other)
Only the three above are binding · all other provisions are non-binding

narration: Confidentiality, expenses, and governing law. These three provisions are legally binding upon execution. Everything else in this Term Sheet is non-binding until the Definitive Documents are executed. All parties shall maintain the existence and content of this Term Sheet, the SAFE, the Definitive Documents, and all due-diligence materials in strict confidence, except as required by law or to professional advisors bound by similar obligations. Each party bears its own expenses in connection with this SAFE financing. The Company is not obligated to reimburse Investor legal expenses. Governing law is Texas. Jurisdiction is the Texas Court of Chancery for internal-affairs disputes and the state and federal courts of Texas for other matters.

title: Acceptance
headline: By countersigning, the Investor confirms intent to proceed.
subhead: Subject to satisfactory completion of the Definitive Documents and accreditation verification. Anticipated SAFE Purchase Amount to be specified by the Investor.

ask_grid.v: BiRAGAS, Inc. · via Sponsor Ayass Bioscience, LLC
Dr. Mohamad Ammar Ayass, MD · Founder & CEO

narration: Acceptance. By countersigning, the Investor confirms intent to proceed with the SAFE financing on the terms set forth in this Term Sheet, subject to satisfactory completion of the Definitive Documents and accreditation verification. Anticipated SAFE Purchase Amount to be specified by the Investor. BiRAGAS Incorporated, in formation, via its Sponsor Ayass Bioscience LLC, signed by Doctor Mohamad Ammar Ayass, MD, Founder and CEO. End of Term Sheet. Version one-point-one R. Confidential. May thirteenth, twenty-twenty-six.
